name: Joe Beck
abstract: Joe Beck (July 29, 1945 – July 22, 2008) was an American jazz guitarist who was active for over 40 years.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: A Night of Genius-Artists Celebrate Mingus

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Bernhardt
about_quotes.evidence: I was in the original fusion band of all time it might have been, with Jeremy Steig and Randy Brecker and myself and Joe Beck.
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Bernhardt interviewed by Monk Rowe, Bearsville, New York, January 3, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Chris Parker
about_quotes.evidence: And my side is with a great bass player Anthony Jackson and we had worked together a lot. And Joe Beck on guitar and Bobby Scott on piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chris Parker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 5, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions 'Joe Beck on Miles in The Sky', but the interviewee (Jack DeJohnette) does not mention Joe Beck, nor does he confirm or deny knowing him. DeJohnette discusses his time in Miles Davis's band but does not reference Beck.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lonnie Liston Smith
about_quotes.evidence: In fact I think you had Joe Beck in your first group? [INTERVIEWEE] Oh yeah, well Bob — Bob wanted Joe in because Joe did some recordings.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lonnie Liston Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 9, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Levin
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Beck played guitar with them somewhere in that period on and off.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Levin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001
